title: calycadenia truncata subsp. scabrella
intro: Calycadenia truncata subsp. scabrella, also known as the Sierra fringe-p Peters or truncata fringe-peters, is a plant species belonging to the daisy family and is native to the western United States. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antibacterial properties. The health benefits of Calycadenia truncata subsp. scabrella include treating wounds, relieving pain, and reducing fever, as well as its potential to lower cholesterol levels and prevent certain types of cancer. Traditional herbal preparations of the plant include making teas, tinctures, and infusions from its leaves and flowers, which can be used to treat various health conditions, although more research is needed to confirm its effectiveness.
